title: Docs: bee-hive SKILL.md onboarding statuses + README updating line
action: Document the shipped behavior (reconstruct from the implemented script, not from intent): (1) bee-hive SKILL.md Onboarding section - the apply flow now also syncs the global bee skill set (one command, D2), the two blocked statuses (blocked_downgrade with --force-downgrade escape, blocked_no_source fail-closed) and what an agent should do on each (surface to the user; never force without an explicit user instruction); (2) README - update the onboarding/updating description with the one-command promise and the downgrade guard, one short paragraph max. Keep both edits inside existing sections; no new ceremony sections. Ordering note (cell review): this cell depends on skill-sync-2 as well - docs describe behavior that has SURVIVED the hermetic suite, not just been written.

truths: SKILL.md names both blocked statuses and the agent response to each
README states that apply updates helpers AND skills together and that downgrades are refused by default
prohibitions: No behavior described that the implemented script does not have (write after reading the final onboard_bee.mjs)
No edits outside skills/bee-hive/SKILL.md and README.md

verify: grep -F 'blocked_downgrade' skills/bee-hive/SKILL.md && grep -F 'blocked_no_source' skills/bee-hive/SKILL.md && grep -iF 'force-downgrade' skills/bee-hive/SKILL.md && grep -i 'downgrade' README.md && grep -iE 'helpers? and (the )?skills|skills? and (the )?helpers' README.md

trace.outcome: Documented shipped skill-sync behavior (reconstructed from the final onboard_bee.mjs, not the plan): SKILL.md Onboarding section now names the one-command promise (--apply syncs helpers + global bee-* skills together), both blocked statuses (blocked_downgrade with the --force-downgrade escape valid only when all three versions resolve numeric; blocked_no_source fail-closed and never forceable) and the blocked_symlink per-skill-item skip, plus the agent response to each (surface to user; never force without explicit instruction). README's onboarding/updating section (skills/bee-hive/scripts/onboard_bee.mjs subsection) gained one paragraph stating apply updates helpers and skills together and that downgrades are refused by default, plus --force-downgrade added to the documented CLI usage line.
trace.friction: README's Status section (decision 0014 bullet, ~line 406) still carries a stale parenthetical claiming 'updating skills requires copying skills/* into ~/.claude/skills + ~/.codex/skills — onboard only refreshes the per-repo .bee/bin/'. This is now false (superseded by skill-sync) but sits outside the cell's designated edit area (onboarding/how-updating-works section only), so left untouched — worth a small follow-up cleanup cell.
trace.verify_output: All 5 grep clauses matched: blocked_downgrade (2 hits) and blocked_no_source (2 hits) and force-downgrade (case-insens, multiple hits) present in SKILL.md; downgrade (multiple hits) and 'helpers and skills together' present in README.md. Exit 0 (chained && all succeeded).
trace.verify_command: grep -F 'blocked_downgrade' skills/bee-hive/SKILL.md && grep -F 'blocked_no_source' skills/bee-hive/SKILL.md && grep -iF 'force-downgrade' skills/bee-hive/SKILL.md && grep -i 'downgrade' README.md && grep -iE 'helpers? and (the )?skills|skills? and (the )?helpers' README.md
